terms.definition: Non-blocking sync warnings; space still usable.
terms.example: Studio - sync warning - 2 saved changes need review
terms.anti_example: Studio space is degraded and unhealthy.
terms.rationale: Operator term from catalog and validation; soften in HUD microcopy and avoid on marketing surfaces.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Seed from alpha source validation and error inventory.

terms.definition: A saved sync change has been held aside until the user or support reviews recovery steps.
terms.anti_example: This quarantined sync envelope is bad.
terms.rationale: Quarantine is a useful diagnostic mechanism name, but product copy should explain the held change and next action.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Marked review because product still needs to decide whether quarantine remains visible to end users.

terms.definition: The current sync and access state for a Heddle space.
terms.example: Review space sync status here, then send diagnostics.
terms.anti_example: Space Health proves the space is healthy.
terms.rationale: Status language is less clinical and handles partial states better than health language.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Marked review because the product name Space Health may be renamed.

terms.definition: A new space created from an import archive for safe inspection or deliberate sync enablement.
terms.example: Restore into a new recovery space.
terms.rationale: The Settings import path creates a new space before writes and is not an existing-destination restore.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Seed from backup/export/import runbook and tests.

terms.definition: A source, space, runtime, or action cannot proceed until a blocker is resolved.
terms.example: Studio - blocked until access is verified.
terms.rationale: Blocked is accurate when paired with a cause and next action.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Seed from source validation and error inventory.

terms.definition: Heddle cannot reach a path, runtime, or selected space right now.
terms.example: Heddle cannot reach this source. Check access, then revalidate.
terms.rationale: Unreachable is concrete and reversible; copy should lead with the next check.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Seed from source and space validation statuses.

terms.definition: A destructive recovery action scoped to local Heddle state on this machine.
terms.example: Reset local state on this machine.
terms.rationale: The copy must make local scope clear and avoid implying account or cloud deletion.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Marked review pending alignment with LocalResetDialog wording.

terms.definition: Internal architecture language for the context/sync layer.
terms.example: The Heddle service keeps syncing in the background.
terms.rationale: Substrate is not plainspoken user-facing language; name the mechanism instead.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Explicitly avoid for user-facing surfaces per post-alpha positioning.

terms.definition: Brand metaphor drawn from heddles, looms, and threads.
terms.example: Heddle holds your work in context.
terms.anti_example: This source fell off the loom.
terms.rationale: The metaphor belongs in brand, illustration, and website narrative, not error, Settings, CLI, or status copy.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Seed from March positioning and illustration guidance.

terms.definition: Puffery shorthand that hides the actual model or agent behavior.
terms.example: This step uses a model to draft a summary.
terms.anti_example: Heddle is an AI-powered context platform.
terms.rationale: Copy guidance bans broad AI claims; specify the capability.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Seed from copy guidance avoid list.

terms.definition: Broad adjective that should be replaced by the specific thing Heddle knows or does.
terms.example: Heddle uses the selected space context for this draft.
terms.rationale: The word overclaims and removes useful detail.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Seed from copy guidance avoid list.

terms.definition: A vague ease claim that should become a concrete setup or background-behavior claim.
terms.anti_example: Heddle seamlessly integrates with your workflow.
terms.rationale: Copy guidance bans seamless because it hides the real interaction and can dismiss user effort.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Seed from copy guidance avoid list.

terms.definition: The Heddle sync unit noun for private, studio, and shared contexts.
terms.example: Your Private space will sync quietly from here.
terms.rationale: Space is already the preferred noun in copy guidance and agent examples.

terms.definition: The action where a machine joins an existing Heddle space.
terms.anti_example: Bind this machine to the workspace.
terms.rationale: Attach is short, concrete, and established in CLI/HUD examples.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Seed from preferred vocabulary and agent examples.

terms.definition: Owner-created shared-space access for a Clerk account or email.
terms.example: Ask a space owner to grant your Clerk account access.
terms.rationale: Account grant distinguishes access policy from invitation messaging.

terms.definition: The background service that runs local Heddle/ccore work.
terms.example: The Heddle service is not responding.
terms.rationale: CLI/docs can use node; HUD and support should name the Heddle service first.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Seed from preferred vocabulary and runtime examples.

terms.definition: Diagnostic proof that a producer, account, or machine can safely read or write a space.
terms.example: Heddle could not verify membership for this space.
terms.rationale: Authority/canonical terms are useful diagnostics but obscure normal user actions.

feedback_log.author: Symphony
feedback_log.note: Added from issue problem statement about internal terms leaking into user-visible strings.
